---
title: "FakeProteinData"
format: html
toc: true
---

# Generate Fake Metadata_df

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(magrittr)

# Set working directory for writing data
data_dir <- "C:/Users/User/Documents/UCSF/BREATH2.Prot.fake/data.prot"
setwd(data_dir)

# Seed spells "BREATH2".. kinda
set.seed(8634782)

# Sets number of samples 
n_definite <- 72
n_noevidence <- 36
n_total = n_definite + n_noevidence

# Metadata
sample_name <- paste0("Patient_", 1:n_total)
LRTI_adjudication <- c(rep("Definite", n_definite), rep("No Evidence", n_noevidence))

# Sex distribution
sex <- sample(c("Male", "Female"), n_total, replace = TRUE)

# Age distribution
age_in_years <- rbeta(n_total, shape1=1.5, shape2=5) * 18

# Proteomic data (200 proteins, normal continuous distribution)
protein_names <- paste0("Protein_", 1:200)
protein_mat <- matrix(
  rnorm(n_total * 200,
        mean = ifelse(rep(LRTI_adjudication, each = 200) == "Definite", 7, 6.5),
        sd = 0.8),
  nrow = n_total,
  ncol = 200,
  dimnames = list(sample_name, protein_names)
)

# Combine into data frame
metadata_df <- data.frame(
  sample_name = sample_name,
  sex = sex,
  age_in_years = age_in_years,
  LRTI_adjudication = LRTI_adjudication,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Save data to metadata data frame and protein matrix
write.csv(metadata_df, file.path(data_dir, "fake_metadata.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(protein_mat, file.path(data_dir, "fake_protein_matrix.csv"), row.names = TRUE)

#print(head(metadata_df))
#print(head(protein_mat[,1:5]))
```



# Generate Folds

```{r}
library(dplyr)

setwd(data_dir)

# Import data
metadata_df <- read.csv("fake_metadata.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

set.seed(8634782)

folds_ok <- FALSE

while (!folds_ok) {
  cv_folds <- metadata_df %>%
    group_by(LRTI_adjudication) %>%
    mutate(fold = sample(rep(1:5, length.out = n()))) %>%
    ungroup()
  
# Check if each fold has at least 7 "No Evidence" samples
  counts <- table(cv_folds$fold, cv_folds$LRTI_adjudication)
  if (all(counts[, "No Evidence"] >= 7)) {
    folds_ok <- TRUE
  }
}

# Save the metadata with folds added
write.csv(cv_folds, file.path(data_dir, "fake_metadata_with_folds.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

#table(cv_folds$LRTI_adjudication, cv_folds$fold)
#print(head(cv_folds))
```

Additional Notes: Minimum of 7 "No Evidence" samples per fold was chosen because ⌊36 / 5 ⌋= 7



# Run LASSO 

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(magrittr)
library(glmnet)
library(tibble)

setwd(data_dir)

set.seed(8634782)

# Import data
metadata_df <- read.csv(file.path(data_dir, "fake_metadata_with_folds.csv"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
protein_mat <- read.csv(file.path(data_dir, "fake_protein_matrix.csv"), row.names = 1)

# Confirm correct data format for regression variables
X <- as.matrix(protein_mat)  
y <- metadata_df$LRTI_adjudication == "Definite"

# Function to extract non-zero values
lasso_coef_df <- function(mod) {
  coefs <- coef(mod, s = "lambda.1se")[, 1]
  
  # Remove intercept
  coefs <- coefs[-1]
  
  # Identify nonzero coefficients
  nonzero <- coefs[coefs != 0]
  
  # If fewer than 3 proteins selected, pick the top 3 by absolute value
  if(length(nonzero) < 3){
    abs_coefs <- abs(coefs)
    top3 <- sort(abs_coefs, decreasing = TRUE)[1:3]
    nonzero <- coefs[names(top3)]
  }
  
  # Return as data.frame
  data.frame(protein = names(nonzero), coef = as.numeric(nonzero))
}

# Function for a single outer CV fold
lasso_cv_outer_fold <- function(test_fold) {
  train <- metadata_df$fold != test_fold
  foldid <- metadata_df$fold[train]
  foldid <- as.integer(as.factor(foldid))

  mod <- glmnet::cv.glmnet(
    X[train, ], y[train],
    family = "binomial",
    foldid = foldid
  )

  # Predictions on test fold
  preds <- predict(mod, X[!train, ], type = "response", s = "lambda.1se")[, 1] %>%
    data.frame(pred = .) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("sample_name")

  # Nonzero coefficients for this fold
  nonzero <- lasso_coef_df(mod) %>%
    mutate(fold = test_fold)

  list(pred = preds, mod = mod, coef = nonzero)
}

# Run LASSO across all folds
lasso_results <- lapply(1:max(metadata_df$fold), lasso_cv_outer_fold)

# Organize predictions and coefficients
lasso_preds <- lasso_results %>%
  lapply(function(x) x$pred) %>%
  bind_rows()

lasso_features <- lasso_results %>%
  lapply(function(x) x$coef) %>%
  bind_rows()

# Save outputs
write.csv(lasso_preds, file.path(data_dir, "lasso_preds.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(lasso_features, file.path(data_dir, "lasso_fold_coefs.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Summary of selected proteins
message("LASSO complete! Number of times each protein was selected across folds:")
print(table(lasso_features$protein))

#print(head(lasso_features))
```

Additional Notes: Fake data with lambda=1 predictably reduces all coefficients to zero. Function sets minimum protein selection of 3 which picks 3 proteins arbitrarily. Real data should behave differently.


# Implement Random Forest

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(magrittr)
library(randomForest)
library(tibble)

setwd(data_dir)

set.seed(8634782)

# Import data
metadata_df <- read.csv(file.path(data_dir, "fake_metadata_with_folds.csv"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
protein_mat  <- read.csv(file.path(data_dir, "fake_protein_matrix.csv"), row.names = 1)

# Import selected proteins per fold
lasso_features <- read.csv(file.path(data_dir, "lasso_fold_coefs.csv"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Ensure row names match sample names (safety step)
rownames(protein_mat) <- metadata_df$sample_name

# Outcome variable
y <- as.factor(metadata_df$LRTI_adjudication)  

# Function to run Random Forest for one outer CV fold
lassoRF_cv_outer_fold <- function(test_fold) {
  
  # Extract proteins selected by LASSO for this fold
  keep <- lasso_features %>%
            filter(fold == test_fold) %>%
            .$protein
  
  # Subset protein matrix to selected proteins; rows = samples, columns = selected proteins
  X <- protein_mat[, keep, drop = FALSE]  
  
  # Boolean for training samples
  train <- metadata_df$fold != test_fold
  
  # Fit Random Forest (10000 Trees)
  rf <- randomForest(X[train, , drop = FALSE], y[train], ntree = 10000)
  
  # Predictions on test fold
  preds <- predict(rf, newdata = X[!train, , drop = FALSE], type = "prob")[, "Definite"]
  
  list(test_fold = test_fold, mod = rf, pred = preds)
}

# Run RF across all folds
cv_list <- lapply(1:max(metadata_df$fold), lassoRF_cv_outer_fold)

# Collect out-of-fold predictions for all samples
rf_preds <- cv_list %>%
  lapply(function(x) {
    data.frame(
      sample_name = rownames(protein_mat)[metadata_df$fold == x$test_fold],
      pred = x$pred
    )
  }) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  left_join(metadata_df %>% select(sample_name, LRTI_adjudication), by = "sample_name") %>%
  relocate(LRTI_adjudication, .after = sample_name)

# Save RF predictions
write.csv(rf_preds, file.path(data_dir, "lassoRF_preds.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Collect RF votes (training set OOB probabilities)
rf_votes <- cv_list %>%
  lapply(function(x) {
    as.data.frame(x$mod$votes) %>%
      tibble::rownames_to_column("sample_name") %>%
      mutate(test_fold = x$test_fold) %>%
      rename(pred = Definite) %>%
      select(test_fold, sample_name, pred)
  }) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  left_join(metadata_df %>% select(sample_name, LRTI_adjudication), by = "sample_name") %>%
  relocate(LRTI_adjudication, .after = sample_name)

# Save votes
write.csv(rf_votes, file.path(data_dir, "lassoRF_votes.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

#print(head(rf_preds))
#print(head(rf_votes))

message("Random Forest complete! Predictions and votes saved.")
```
